__NOTOC__ {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Center Game Accepted| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |ql|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl| |kl|bl|nl|rl|= | |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C22]] |parent=[[Chess/Open Game|Open Game]] }} ==Center Game Accepted== In the Center Game, White is happy with himself for knocking out Black's only foothold in the center square, e5. At the time, all four central squares are controlled by White. This may seem like a huge advantage for White, but if Black simply plays Nc6, the queen is forced to move. Conversely, it may seem like a bad idea to develop the queen before any other pieces; however, Black can get into trouble if careless. === Black's responses === Usually, Black responds to the center game by playing '''[[/3...Nc6/]]''' forcing White's queen to leave its central outpost. ==Theory table== '''1. e4 e5 2. d4 exd4 3. Qxd4''' {| class="wikitable" |- !   !! 3 !! 4 !! 5 !! 6 !! Evaluation |- ! [[Chess/Center Game|Center Game]] | '''...
